text.title: Action and DTO Patterns
text.summary: Single-purpose Action classes with DB::transaction, and readonly DTO with fromRequest factory method.

rule.statement: One Action = one verb (CreateUserAction, not UserAction::create). Actions must return a domain entity or result object — never void.
rule.rationale: Named-verb actions map 1:1 to SDD tasks and are independently testable. Returning void prevents downstream composition by queue jobs or console commands.

rule.statement: Wrap multi-write Actions in DB::transaction(); Eloquent does not auto-run transactions across create + relation-attach chains.
rule.rationale: A failed notification or role assignment after a successful user create leaves orphaned state without rollback.

example.note: DTO fromRequest() is the single point of truth. Never reach into $request->input() inside an Action — it couples the Action to HTTP.

antipattern.description: Action calling another Action inline in the same request transaction.
antipattern.reason: Multi-Action orchestration belongs in a Service class. Actions calling Actions span unclear transaction boundaries and become untestable in isolation.
